verdict.answer_first: Both answer immediately; A is slightly sharper with "Yes — the main problem is using floating-point math for money" while B also leads with "The main problem is using floating-point arithmetic".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more clearly with "Fix: do money math in integer minor units (cents)"; B ends by handing off with "Want me to apply a fix".
verdict.fabrication: B makes a questionable claim: "or it's a string like \"9.99\", the result silently becomes NaN"; JS may concatenate instead. A has no material fabrication.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives a replacement function under "Fix:" and names the preferred source-level change.
